_comment_purpose: FALLBACK MODE — only use when blocking-task path can't be relied upon (intermittent agent connectivity, very long network round-trips that exceed worker's 30s execution limit when summed across mappings, or for whatever reason you don't want the worker to actively poke the agent on every tick). Uses N agent crons (one per agent uuid, distinct names) + 1 server cron that reads crontab_result via cron_name_fetch_ip in env.MAPPINGS. The recommended path is `nodeget-cron.example.json` — single server cron + agent_uuid directly in MAPPINGS.
_comment_substitute: Replace placeholders: NODEGET_API_TOKEN, AGENT_UUID_HOME, AGENT_UUID_NAS, WORKER_NAME.
_comment_cron_format: 6-segment: sec min hr day mon weekday. Agent ip crons fire at :00, server worker cron at :30 so the agent results land before the server reads them.

server_worker_cron_all._comment: One server cron iterates over every mapping in env.MAPPINGS. js_worker[1] is the `params` arg to onCron — leave {} for normal scheduled sync.

_optional_only_one_mapping._comment: Want a separate cadence for one mapping (e.g. NAS sync less often)? Drop the all-cron above and run per-mapping server crons with `only`:

_optional_daily_force._comment: Once a day at 03:00, force a re-push for all mappings (catches silent CF dashboard edits faster than the in-worker HEARTBEAT_HOURS path).
